title: PS-69892: Token exchange
author: Daniel-Weng-TC1

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Implements PS-69892 by adding a WebUI API endpoint and supporting backend/identity plumbing to exchange an agent-provided JWT assertion for a ProjectSight-Web access token, gated by configured audience.

**Changes:**
- Added `ExchangeAgentTokenForProjectSightToken` API endpoint in WebUI.
- Implemented token exchange flow in `AgenticPlatformSystem`, including audience allow-list check and token issuance via `IdentityService`.
- Extended JWT identity extraction to optionally capture `aud` claims and added unit tests for key failure paths and audience validation.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| Core/Web Layer/WebUI/api/AgenticPlatformSystemController.cs | Adds POST endpoint that reads `x-jwt-assertion` and returns exchanged token payload/status codes. |
| Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/AgenticPlatformSystem.cs | Implements token exchange business logic, audience gating, and response/result models. |
| Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/Services/Identity/V4/JWTService.cs | Extends identity extraction to optionally parse audience (`aud`) claims for the exchange path. |
| Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/Services/Identity/TIDSupport.cs | Adds optional `checkExtra` parameter to propagate extra-claim parsing to JWTService. |
| Core/Common/Components/User/UserIdents.cs | Adds audience storage on the identity object for downstream authorization checks. |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.UnitTests/AgenticPlatform/AgenticPlatformTests.cs | Adds unit tests covering token exchange failure cases and audience allow-list behavior. |
</details>
reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.
reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

review_comments.body: Audience (`aud`) is only collected conditionally, but the loop later breaks early once the core identity claims are found. If `aud` claims appear later in the token, `audiences` will never be populated and token exchange can incorrectly fail authorization.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `Audiences` is exposed as a public mutable `List<Guid>` field, which allows callers to mutate identity state after construction and breaks the otherwise read-only shape of this identity object. Prefer an encapsulated read-only surface and copy the incoming list to avoid aliasing.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Concatenating multiple header values via `string.Join` makes the accepted token format ambiguous. It’s safer to treat `x-jwt-assertion` as a single value (or explicitly pick the first non-empty value) and reject/ignore additional values deterministically.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Use the `SystemBase` `_dbHelperFactory` field instead of instantiating a new `DBHelperFactory` here. Creating a new factory bypasses dependency injection used in unit tests and can lead to inconsistent DB helper configuration.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: The `catch` for the token exchange DB call swallows exceptions without logging. Returning a 500 is fine, but without logging it will be hard to diagnose operational issues in production.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: This block does not compile: the `try` statement is missing braces, the `using` statement is missing braces, and `tokenResult` is referenced without being declared/assigned. The token exchange implementation needs to be completed and the control flow structured with proper `{}` blocks.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `idents.Audiences` is declared as `IReadOnlyList<Guid>` (see UserIdents), but `HasApprovedAudience` currently takes `List<Guid>`, so this call will not compile. Update the parameter type to accept `IReadOnlyList<Guid>` (or `IEnumerable<Guid>`).
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: When `checkExtra` is true, the loop can break before later `aud` claims are encountered because the early-break condition only checks the core identity claims. Claim order is not guaranteed, so this can silently drop audience IDs and cause token-exchange authorization to fail.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: The method comment says exceptions are handled here and translated to HTTP status codes, but `InitializeRequestAsync(...)` can still throw and will be handled by the global exception handler (per the controller summary). Either wrap the whole method in try/catch (and translate) or update the comment to match the actual behavior.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: The summary contains an incomplete/unclear sentence ("like PS MCP server only on Prod.") which reads as a leftover note rather than documentation. This should be clarified or removed to avoid confusion for API consumers and future maintainers.
review_comments.author: Copilot
